document.title: Usability Engineering Report For Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability engineering report for HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG digital flat panel X-ray detectors.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents usability engineering, intended use, user profiles, hazards, risk controls, verification, and validation results for the detector.
keyPoints: The report identifies the products as HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG digital flat panel X-ray detectors.
The report references EN ISO 14971:2012 and EN 62366:2008 as normative references.
The intended purpose is a digital imaging solution for general radiographic systems for human anatomy, replacing film or screen based radiographic systems in general purpose diagnostic procedures.
The devices are not to be used for mammography and infant or pregnant women.
The intended user is a medical professional with a professional license, familiar with the user manual, with Korean language understanding.
Usability validation used five intended users and concluded all users were accepted as green zones.

evidence.claim: The document is a usability engineering report for a digital flat panel X-ray detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Engineering Report For Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The report number and revision are A-UER-001 Rev.4.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-UER-001 (Rev.4)

evidence.claim: The report identifies HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG as product models.
evidence.evidenceText: HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG

evidence.claim: The document approval date shown in the signature table is DEC/12/2020.
evidence.evidenceText: Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu DEC/12/2020

evidence.claim: Rev.4 is dated DEC/12/2020 and describes correction of audit points.
evidence.evidenceText: 4 DEC/12/2020 Correction of audit Points

evidence.claim: The normative references include EN ISO 14971:2012.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2012, Medical devices – Application of risk management to medical devices

evidence.claim: The normative references include EN 62366:2008.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62366:2008 - Medical devices - Application of usability engineering to medical devices

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is a digital imaging solution for general radiographic systems for human anatomy.
evidence.evidenceText: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are indicated for digital imaging solution designed for general radiographic system for human anatomy.

evidence.claim: The device is intended to replace film or screen based radiographic systems in general purpose diagnostic procedures.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film or screen based radiographic systems in all general purpose diagnostic procedures.

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography and infant or pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography and infant or pregnant women.

evidence.claim: The patient population includes radiographic imaging of human body parts such as head, chest, spinal, abdominal, hand, foot, and internal organs.
evidence.evidenceText: Radiographic imaging of human body parts such as head, Chest, spinal, abdominal, particular, hand, foot and other internal organs.

evidence.claim: The intended user is a medical professional with a professional license who is familiar with the user manual.
evidence.evidenceText: Education: A medical professional with a professional license Knowledge: Conditions that satisfy both of the following two conditions - A medical professional with a professional license - familiar with user manual

evidence.claim: The intended user's language understanding is Korean.
evidence.evidenceText: Language Understanding: Korean

evidence.claim: Operating condition limits are ambient temperature +5 to 40℃ and relative humidity 30 to 75 RH % without condensation.
evidence.evidenceText: Operating condition Limit - Ambient temperature range:+5 ~ 40℃ - Relative humidity:30 ~ 75 RH % without condensation

evidence.claim: Storage condition limits are ambient temperature -10 to +50℃ and relative humidity 10 to 80 RH % without condensation.
evidence.evidenceText: Storage condition Limit - Ambient temperature range:-10 ~ + 50℃ - Relative humidity:10 ~ 80 RH % without condensation

evidence.claim: The applied parts classification is not applied part.
evidence.evidenceText: 6.1.5 Classification of APPLIED PARTS Not applied part

evidence.claim: Frequently used functions include connecting the power cord, data cable, LAN cable, trigger cable, and communication cable.
evidence.evidenceText: Connecting the power cord to the power supply; Connecting the Data Cable to the Power Supply and the Detector; Connecting the LAN cable to the power supply; Trigger cable connection to power supply; LAN cable and PC main body connection; Connecting the communication cable to the power supply

evidence.claim: The document identifies known or foreseeable hazards using IEC62336 Annex E questions.
evidence.evidenceText: Questions that can be used to identify medical device characteristics. (IEC62336 Annex E)

evidence.claim: The equipment is susceptible to environmental influences.
evidence.evidenceText: E.2.5 (62366) Is the MEDICAL DEVICE susceptible to environmental influences? A The equipment is susceptible to environmental influences.

evidence.claim: The device is used with a power supply.
evidence.evidenceText: E.2.6 (62366) Are there essential consumables or accessories associated with the MEDICAL DEVICE? A It is used with power supply.

evidence.claim: The device does not need maintenance but needs calibration.
evidence.evidenceText: E.2.7 (62366) Is maintenance and/or calibration necessary? A This device does not need maintenance but need calibration

evidence.claim: Primary operating functions include power cord connection, cable connection, main power button action, and software execution.
evidence.evidenceText: Power cord connection; cable connection; Main Power Button Action; Software execution

evidence.claim: Usability validation planned five typical intended users.
evidence.evidenceText: Typical Intended User (Test Staff) : 5 persons

evidence.claim: Validation test staff were intended users who can use radiation devices, such as doctors or radiologists.
evidence.evidenceText: The level of knowledge education for intended users: those who can use radiation devices (doctors or radiologists)

evidence.claim: All users were successful in the acceptable range and accepted as green zones.
evidence.evidenceText: Tests were conducted according to the test methods established in the usability validation plan, and all users were successful in the acceptable range of the test criteria. Therefore, all users were accepted as green zones.

evidence.claim: Accompanying documents include the Usability Engineering Plan, Risk management report, and TCF.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Engineering Plan (A-UEP-001) Risk management report (A-RMR-0-01) TCF (TCF-001)

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is ambiguous: sourcePath indicates CE MDD, source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, and the document text does not identify FDA, CE authority, notified body, or jurisdiction.
Potential date conflict: sourcePath filename includes 2020.12.10, while the document signature table and revision history state DEC/12/2020.

Conflicting evidence: Section 6.11.1 says the device applies constant heat to the human body to relieve muscle pain, which conflicts with the report title and intended purpose for a digital flat panel X-ray detector.
Conflicting evidence: Section 6.11.2 references usability of a medical warmer (BPD-1700 G) product while also referring to detector HAD1717MG and HAD17MC.
Manufacturer or legal entity name is not present in the provided source text.
The document cites IEC62336 Annex E, which may be an OCR or transcription issue because the surrounding context references EN 62366:2008.
